title: Deceased-patient / memorial-registry consent pattern + ethics notes
context: Deceased-patient and memorial-registry data are a lower-barrier entry point for rare-cancer registries, but 'deceased-only' does not mean consent-free or ethics-free. HIPAA protects decedent PHI for 50 years after death (§164.502(f)); GDPR Recital 27 excludes deceased persons from scope but several member states legislate post-mortem data protection; surviving-relative privacy, genetic-relatedness implications, and family/community dignity obligations persist. This pattern must treat deceased data with its own ethical framework, not as a loophole. Depends on rare-cancer-registry-templates-consent-001. Bioethics + patient-advocate sign-off required.
objective: Author the deceased-patient / memorial-registry consent pattern (`governance/consent/deceased-memorial-pattern.md`) covering: (1) the HIPAA 50-year decedent rule (§164.502(f)), (2) GDPR Recital 27 and EU member-state post-mortem data protection caveats, (3) next-of-kin / authorised-representative consent path and documentation, (4) surviving-relative privacy and genetic-implication notes, and (5) ethics notes establishing that deceased-only is not an ethics-free shortcut. Carries 'informational, not legal advice' framing throughout.
acceptanceCriteria: Pattern includes the HIPAA 50-year decedent rule (§164.502(f)) with primary-source citation.
Pattern includes GDPR Recital 27 and notes EU member-state post-mortem data protection laws with primary-source citations and jurisdictional caveats.
Next-of-kin / authorised-representative consent path and required documentation are specified.
Surviving-relative privacy and genetic-relatedness implications are addressed with ethics notes.
Pattern explicitly states that deceased-only is not an ethics-free or consent-free shortcut: obligations persist.
Every page carries 'Informational — not legal advice; consult your IRB/REC, DPO, and counsel.'
Bioethics + patient-advocate sign-off recorded; sign-off is version-scoped.
output: A `governance/consent/deceased-memorial-pattern.md` document covering HIPAA/GDPR decedent nuances, next-of-kin consent, and ethics notes, committed via PR only after bioethics + advocate sign-off.
